sourceText: John Chilembwe
translatedText: John Chilembwe

sourceText: The oppression and racial injustice of colonial rule.
translatedText: Ukandamizaji na dhuluma ya rangi ya utawala wa kikoloni.

sourceText: Building resistance through education, faith, and ultimately, armed rebellion.
translatedText: Kujenga upinzani kupitia elimu, imani, na hatimaye, uasi wa kutumia silaha.

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwanzo wa Karne ya 20

sourceText: Cetshwayo kaMpande
translatedText: Ushauri wa Mpande

sourceText: Losing my kingdom and the independence of my people to British imperial expansion and internal strife.
translatedText: Kupoteza ufalme wangu na uhuru wa watu wangu kwa upanuzi wa kifalme wa Uingereza na ugomvi wa ndani.

sourceText: I traveled to London to plead my case to Queen Victoria, successfully arguing for my restoration to the throne, albeit with diminished power.
translatedText: Nilisafiri hadi London kutetea kesi yangu kwa Malkia Victoria, nikibishana kwa mafanikio ya kurejeshwa kwangu kwenye kiti cha enzi, ingawa kwa nguvu ndogo.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Being posthumously depicted as a cruel tyrant by later historians like Herodotus, and having the true purpose of my pyramid misunderstood.
translatedText: Kuonyeshwa baada ya kifo kama dhalimu katili na wanahistoria wa baadaye kama Herodotus, na kutoeleweka kwa madhumuni ya kweli ya piramidi yangu.

sourceText: The Great Pyramid itself stands as the ultimate testament to my organizational power, the prosperity of my kingdom, and our unshakeable faith in the eternal afterlife.
translatedText: Piramidi Kuu yenyewe inasimama kama ushuhuda wa mwisho wa uwezo wangu wa shirika, ustawi wa ufalme wangu, na imani yetu isiyotikisika katika maisha ya baadaye ya milele.

sourceText: Ancient Egypt, 4th Dynasty
translatedText: Misri ya Kale, nasaba ya 4

sourceText: Constant internal power struggles with my own family for the throne and the ever-present threat of Roman annexation looming over my kingdom.
translatedText: Mapambano ya mara kwa mara ya mamlaka ya ndani na familia yangu mwenyewe kwa ajili ya kiti cha enzi na tishio la kila mara la kunyakuliwa kwa Warumi linaloukabili ufalme wangu.

sourceText: By forging powerful personal and political alliances with Rome's most influential leaders, Julius Caesar and Mark Antony, I secured my throne and prolonged Egypt's independence.
translatedText: Kwa kuunda ushirikiano wenye nguvu wa kibinafsi na wa kisiasa na viongozi mashuhuri wa Roma, Julius Caesar na Mark Antony, nilipata kiti changu cha enzi na kuongeza muda wa uhuru wa Misri.

sourceText: Ptolemaic Period
translatedText: Kipindi cha Ptolemaic

sourceText: A homeland torn apart by civil war, the hypocrisy of European missionaries, and the conflict between traditional beliefs and Christianity.
translatedText: Nchi iliyosambaratishwa na vita vya wenyewe kwa wenyewe, unafiki wa wamishonari wa Ulaya, na mzozo kati ya imani za kimapokeo na Ukristo.

sourceText: Forging a new religious movement from visions of Saint Anthony, uniting the Kongo people to reoccupy and rebuild their ancient capital.
translatedText: Kuanzisha vuguvugu jipya la kidini kutoka kwa maono ya Mtakatifu Anthony, kuunganisha watu wa Kongo kuchukua tena na kujenga mji mkuu wao wa zamani.

sourceText: Late 17th - Early 18th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 17 - Mapema karne ya 18

sourceText: Tariq ibn Ziyad
translatedText: Tariq ibn Ziyad

sourceText: Conflict and jealousy from his superior, Musa ibn Nusayr, and being recalled to Damascus, where he lived out his days in obscurity.
translatedText: Migogoro na wivu kutoka kwa mkuu wake, Musa ibn Nusayr, na kurejeshwa Damascus, ambako aliishi siku zake za giza.

sourceText: His military genius and bold leadership were immortalized in history, and the name 'Gibraltar' ensures his legacy endures.
translatedText: Umahiri wake wa kijeshi na uongozi shupavu haukufa katika historia, na jina 'Gibraltar' linahakikisha urithi wake unadumu.

sourceText: Early 8th Century
translatedText: Mapema karne ya 8

sourceText: Taytu Betul
translatedText: Hiyo ni kweli

sourceText: The colonial ambitions of Italy and the constant threat from internal political rivals.
translatedText: Matarajio ya kikoloni ya Italia na tishio la mara kwa mara kutoka kwa wapinzani wa ndani wa kisiasa.

sourceText: Defended Ethiopia's sovereignty through brilliant diplomacy, military insight, and a strong partnership with her husband, Emperor Menelik II.
translatedText: Alitetea uhuru wa Ethiopia kupitia diplomasia nzuri, maarifa ya kijeshi, na ushirikiano mkubwa na mumewe, Mfalme Menelik II.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Abubakar Tafawa Balewa
translatedText: Abubakar Tafawa Balewa

sourceText: Navigating intense regional and ethnic rivalries while building a new nation.
translatedText: Kupitia mashindano makali ya kikanda na kikabila huku tukijenga taifa jipya.

sourceText: Through diplomacy, compromise, and advocating for a federal system to accommodate diversity.
translatedText: Kupitia diplomasia, maelewano, na kutetea mfumo wa shirikisho ili kushughulikia utofauti.

sourceText: 20th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 20

sourceText: The relentless invasions by the Assyrian Empire, which ultimately forced my retreat from Lower Egypt.
translatedText: Uvamizi usiokoma wa Milki ya Ashuru, ambao hatimaye ulilazimisha kutoroka kwangu kutoka Misri ya Chini.

sourceText: By withdrawing to my Nubian homeland, I preserved the heart of the Kushite kingdom and ensured its culture would endure.
translatedText: Kwa kujiondoa katika nchi yangu ya Wanubi, nilihifadhi moyo wa ufalme wa Kushite na kuhakikisha utamaduni wake ungedumu.

sourceText: 7th Century BCE
translatedText: Karne ya 7 KK

sourceText: Witnessing the moral corruption of Roman society and the brutal, unjust persecution of fellow Christians.
translatedText: Kushuhudia upotovu wa kiadili wa jamii ya Waroma na mateso ya kikatili na yasiyo ya haki ya Wakristo wenzao.

sourceText: Through prolific writing, he defended Christianity (apologetics) and forged a new Latin theological vocabulary to articulate and preserve the faith.
translatedText: Kupitia maandishi mengi, alitetea Ukristo (apologetics) na kutengeneza msamiati mpya wa kitheolojia wa Kilatini ili kueleza na kuhifadhi imani.

sourceText: Roman Empire
translatedText: Ufalme wa Kirumi

sourceText: Tewodros II
translatedText: Theodore II

sourceText: A fractured empire and encroaching foreign powers.
translatedText: Milki iliyovunjika na kuingilia mamlaka ya kigeni.

sourceText: Reunification through military conquest and centralizing reforms.
translatedText: Kuunganishwa tena kwa ushindi wa kijeshi na mageuzi ya kati.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Tutankhamun
translatedText: Tutankhamun

sourceText: Ascending the throne as a child amidst religious turmoil, I was burdened by political intrigue and suffered from numerous physical ailments throughout my short life.
translatedText: Nilipopanda kiti cha enzi nikiwa mtoto katikati ya misukosuko ya kidini, nililemewa na fitina za kisiasa na kuteseka kutokana na magonjwa mengi ya kimwili katika maisha yangu mafupi.

sourceText: I worked to restore stability by reversing my father Akhenaten's radical monotheistic reforms and re-establishing the traditional pantheon of Egyptian gods.
translatedText: Nilifanya kazi ili kurejesha uthabiti kwa kubadili mageuzi makubwa ya baba yangu Akhenaten ya kuamini Mungu mmoja na kuanzisha upya miungu ya kitamaduni ya miungu ya Misri.

sourceText: New Kingdom, 18th Dynasty
translatedText: Ufalme Mpya, Nasaba ya 18

sourceText: The pressure of British imperialism, the division of my kingdom, and a long 28-year exile.
translatedText: Shinikizo la ubeberu wa Uingereza, mgawanyiko wa ufalme wangu, na uhamisho wa muda mrefu wa miaka 28.

sourceText: Returning from exile to a hero's welcome, being restored as Kumasihene, and becoming a symbol of Ashanti unity and identity.
translatedText: Kurudi kutoka uhamishoni hadi kukaribishwa kwa shujaa, kurejeshwa kama Kumasihene, na kuwa ishara ya umoja na utambulisho wa Ashanti.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: The political fragmentation of Egypt and the rise of ambitious local rulers who threatened traditional religious practices.
translatedText: Mgawanyiko wa kisiasa wa Misri na kuongezeka kwa watawala wa ndani wenye tamaa ambao walitishia mazoea ya kidini ya jadi.

sourceText: By launching a holy war in the name of Amun, defeating the northern coalition, and reunifying Egypt under his Kushite rule.
translatedText: Kwa kuanzisha vita vitakatifu kwa jina la Amun, kuushinda muungano wa kaskazini, na kuunganisha tena Misri chini ya utawala wake wa Kushi.

sourceText: Third Intermediate Period of Egypt
translatedText: Kipindi cha Tatu cha Kati cha Misri

sourceText: Herbert Macaulay
translatedText: Herbert Macaulay

sourceText: Endured constant surveillance, multiple imprisonments by the colonial government, and faced opposition and misunderstanding from some of his own countrymen.
translatedText: Alistahimili ufuatiliaji wa mara kwa mara, kufungwa mara nyingi na serikali ya kikoloni, na kukabiliwa na upinzani na kutokuelewana kutoka kwa baadhi ya wananchi wake.

sourceText: Persisted by founding newspapers, establishing the first Nigerian political party, and using his powerful oratory to unite Nigerians against colonial oppression.
translatedText: Alidumu kwa kuanzisha magazeti, kuanzisha chama cha kwanza cha kisiasa cha Nigeria, na kutumia hotuba yake yenye nguvu kuwaunganisha Wanigeria dhidi ya ukandamizaji wa wakoloni.

sourceText: Late 19th - Mid 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - katikati ya karne ya 20

sourceText: Hendrik Witbooi
translatedText: Hendrik Witbooi

sourceText: The anguish of foreign invasion, internal tribal conflicts, and the ultimate struggle against colonial subjugation.
translatedText: Maumivu ya uvamizi wa kigeni, migogoro ya ndani ya kikabila, na mapambano ya mwisho dhidi ya kutiishwa kwa wakoloni.

sourceText: By uniting disparate Namibian peoples against a common enemy, turning from a tactical ally of the Germans to their most determined opponent for freedom.
translatedText: Kwa kuwaunganisha watu wa Namibia waliotofautiana dhidi ya adui mmoja, wakigeuka kutoka kwa mshirika wa mbinu wa Wajerumani hadi mpinzani wao aliyedhamiria zaidi kwa uhuru.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Huda Sha'arawi
translatedText: Huda Sha'arawi

sourceText: A secluded harem life from childhood, an early arranged marriage, and the societal and legal restrictions placed upon women.
translatedText: Maisha ya maharimu yaliyotengwa tangu utotoni, ndoa iliyopangwa mapema, na vikwazo vya kijamii na kisheria vilivyowekwa kwa wanawake.

sourceText: Self-empowerment through education and reading, founding the Egyptian Feminist Union by uniting with other women, and leading public acts of defiance to create change.
translatedText: Kujiwezesha kupitia elimu na kusoma, kuanzisha Umoja wa Wanawake wa Misri kwa kuungana na wanawake wengine, na kuongoza vitendo vya ukaidi vya umma kuleta mabadiliko.

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwanzo wa Karne ya 20

sourceText: Deep inner conflict and anguish from a hedonistic youth, adherence to Manichaeism, and the early death of his son.
translatedText: Migogoro ya ndani ya ndani na uchungu kutoka kwa vijana wa hedonistic, kufuata Manichaeism, na kifo cha mapema cha mtoto wake.

sourceText: Converted to Christianity through his mother Monica's prayers and Bishop Ambrose's sermons, finding intellectual and spiritual answers in God's grace by dedicating his life to theology and philosophy.
translatedText: Aligeuzwa kuwa Mkristo kupitia maombi ya mama yake Monica na mahubiri ya Askofu Ambrose, akipata majibu ya kiakili na kiroho katika neema ya Mungu kwa kujitolea maisha yake kwa teolojia na falsafa.

sourceText: Late Roman Empire
translatedText: Marehemu Dola ya Kirumi

sourceText: J. E. Casely Hayford
translatedText: J. E. Casely Hayford

sourceText: Witnessing the erosion of traditional African institutions and rights under colonial rule.
translatedText: Kushuhudia mmomonyoko wa taasisi na haki za jadi za Kiafrika chini ya utawala wa kikoloni.

sourceText: Advocating for African self-determination through political organization, journalism, and literature.
translatedText: Kutetea uhuru wa Waafrika kupitia shirika la kisiasa, uandishi wa habari na fasihi.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Frantz Fanon
translatedText: Frantz Fanon

sourceText: He grappled with the internalised racism and identity crises born from a colonial upbringing, and was profoundly traumatized by the brutality he witnessed as a psychiatrist during the Algerian War.
translatedText: Alikabiliana na ubaguzi wa ndani na migogoro ya utambulisho uliozaliwa kutokana na malezi ya kikoloni, na alihuzunishwa sana na ukatili aliouona akiwa daktari wa magonjwa ya akili wakati wa Vita vya Algeria.

sourceText: He channeled his experiences into his psychoanalytic practice and revolutionary writings, theorizing the path to decolonization and advocating for the liberation of oppressed peoples worldwide.
translatedText: Alielekeza uzoefu wake katika mazoezi yake ya uchanganuzi wa kisaikolojia na maandishi ya kimapinduzi, akielezea njia ya uondoaji wa ukoloni na kutetea ukombozi wa watu waliokandamizwa ulimwenguni kote.

sourceText: 20th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 20

sourceText: The pervasive 'color line' of racial discrimination and the social and intellectual isolation it created. He also faced opposition from both white society and other Black leaders.
translatedText: 'Mstari wa rangi' ulioenea wa ubaguzi wa rangi na utengaji wa kijamii na kiakili ulioanzisha. Pia alikabiliwa na upinzani kutoka kwa jamii ya wazungu na viongozi wengine weusi.

sourceText: Through rigorous scholarship, prolific writing, and tireless activism, he confronted racial injustice, co-founding the NAACP and championing Pan-Africanism to unite people of African descent globally.
translatedText: Kupitia usomi mkali, uandishi wa kina, na uharakati usiochoka, alikabiliana na dhuluma ya rangi, akianzisha NAACP na kutetea Pan-Africanism kuunganisha watu wa asili ya Kiafrika duniani kote.

sourceText: Late 19th - Mid 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - katikati ya karne ya 20

sourceText: George Gemistos Plethon
translatedText: George Gemistos Plethon

sourceText: Disillusionment with the declining Byzantine Empire and the rigid doctrines of the Orthodox Church.
translatedText: Kukatishwa tamaa na kupungua kwa Milki ya Byzantium na mafundisho magumu ya Kanisa la Othodoksi.

sourceText: Reviving ancient Greek philosophy, especially Plato's thought, to propose a new social and political model.
translatedText: Kufufua falsafa ya kale ya Kigiriki, hasa mawazo ya Plato, kupendekeza mtindo mpya wa kijamii na kisiasa.

sourceText: 15th Century Byzantine Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Byzantine ya Karne ya 15

sourceText: Nikolai Gogol
translatedText: Nikolai Gogol

sourceText: Profound religious turmoil, mental instability, and a crippling self-doubt about the moral purpose of his own literary creations.
translatedText: Msukosuko mkubwa wa kidini, kuyumba kiakili, na hali ya kutokuwa na shaka yenye kulemaza kuhusu kusudi la kiadili la ubunifu wake mwenyewe wa fasihi.

sourceText: He channeled his anguish into satirical masterpieces, but ultimately succumbed to religious fanaticism and self-destructive asceticism in his final years.
translatedText: Alielekeza uchungu wake katika kazi bora za kejeli, lakini hatimaye alishindwa na ushupavu wa kidini na kujinyima moyo kwa kujiangamiza katika miaka yake ya mwisho.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Nikolay Muravyov-Amursky
translatedText: Nikolay Muravyov-Amursky

sourceText: Facing bureaucratic inertia from St. Petersburg, the immense logistical challenges of a vast and undeveloped territory, and the delicate diplomatic dance with a weakened but still formidable Qing China.
translatedText: Inakabiliwa na hali ya urasimu kutoka St.

sourceText: By acting with decisive, often unilateral, authority granted by the Tsar, and by fostering a spirit of pioneering and settlement, I pushed through my vision for a Russian Pacific.
translatedText: Kwa kutenda kwa mamlaka madhubuti, mara nyingi ya upande mmoja, yaliyotolewa na Tsar, na kwa kukuza roho ya upainia na makazi, nilisukuma maono yangu kwa Pasifiki ya Urusi.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Nikolai Vavilov
translatedText: Nikolay Vavilov

sourceText: Persecuted and imprisoned for his scientific beliefs, which contradicted the state-sponsored theories of Trofim Lysenko.
translatedText: Kuteswa na kufungwa kwa imani yake ya kisayansi, ambayo ilipingana na nadharia zilizofadhiliwa na serikali za Trofim Lysenko.

sourceText: His legacy was posthumously restored, and his work on crop diversity and seed banking is now considered fundamental to global food security.
translatedText: Urithi wake ulirejeshwa baada ya kifo chake, na kazi yake ya uanuwai wa mazao na kuhifadhi mbegu sasa inachukuliwa kuwa msingi kwa usalama wa chakula duniani.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Nikolai Przhevalsky
translatedText: Nikolai Przhevalsky

sourceText: Battling harsh terrain, extreme weather, hostility from local populations, and diseases contracted during his expeditions.
translatedText: Kupambana na hali mbaya ya hewa, hali mbaya ya hewa, uhasama kutoka kwa wakazi wa eneo hilo, na magonjwa aliyopata wakati wa safari zake.

sourceText: Overcame these challenges through sheer willpower, meticulous planning, and an unyielding passion for discovery and the unknown.
translatedText: Alishinda changamoto hizi kupitia utashi kamili, kupanga kwa uangalifu, na shauku isiyobadilika ya ugunduzi na mambo yasiyojulikana.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Danylo Halytskyi
translatedText: Danylo Halytskyi

sourceText: Exile in his youth after his father's death and constant rebellions from internal boyars who opposed the unification of his lands.
translatedText: Uhamisho katika ujana wake baada ya kifo cha baba yake na uasi wa mara kwa mara kutoka kwa wavulana wa ndani ambao walipinga kuunganishwa kwa ardhi yake.

sourceText: Through relentless military campaigns and shrewd diplomacy, he defeated his rivals, balanced the powers of the Mongols and Western Europe, and was crowned king.
translatedText: Kupitia kampeni za kijeshi zisizokoma na diplomasia ya busara, aliwashinda wapinzani wake, akasawazisha mamlaka ya Wamongolia na Ulaya Magharibi, na kutawazwa mfalme.

sourceText: 13th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 13

sourceText: Inheriting a kingdom ravaged by Seljuk Turk invasions and facing constant rebellions from powerful feudal lords.
translatedText: Kurithi ufalme ulioharibiwa na uvamizi wa Waturuki wa Seljuk na kukabiliwa na uasi wa mara kwa mara kutoka kwa watawala wakuu wenye nguvu.

sourceText: Through sweeping military, administrative, and church reforms, and a decisive victory at the Battle of Didgori.
translatedText: Kupitia mageuzi makubwa ya kijeshi, utawala, na kanisa, na ushindi mnono kwenye Vita vya Didgori.

sourceText: 11th-12th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 11-12

sourceText: Rabban Bar Sauma
translatedText: Rabban Bar Sauma

sourceText: Unable to complete his original spiritual pilgrimage to Jerusalem due to political turmoil, he was thrust into the world of international diplomacy.
translatedText: Hakuweza kukamilisha hija yake ya awali ya kiroho ya Yerusalemu kutokana na misukosuko ya kisiasa, alisukumwa katika ulimwengu wa diplomasia ya kimataifa.

sourceText: He embraced his new role as a divine mission, dedicating himself to fostering an alliance between East and West and documenting his extraordinary travels.
translatedText: Alikubali jukumu lake jipya kama misheni ya kimungu, akijitolea kukuza muungano kati ya Mashariki na Magharibi na kuandika safari zake za ajabu.

sourceText: 13th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 13

sourceText: Lesya Ukrainka
translatedText: Lesya Ukrainka

sourceText: A lifelong battle with bone tuberculosis, causing constant pain and limiting mobility, alongside the political oppression of Ukrainian culture by the Russian Empire.
translatedText: Vita vya maisha na kifua kikuu cha mfupa, na kusababisha maumivu ya mara kwa mara na kupunguza uhamaji, pamoja na ukandamizaji wa kisiasa wa utamaduni wa Kiukreni na Dola ya Kirusi.

sourceText: She channeled her physical and emotional suffering into powerful literary works, becoming a symbol of Ukrainian resilience. Her extensive travels for medical treatment also broadened her intellectual horizons, enriching her writing.
translatedText: Alielekeza mateso yake ya kimwili na kihisia katika kazi zenye nguvu za fasihi, na kuwa ishara ya uthabiti wa Kiukreni. Safari zake nyingi za matibabu pia zilipanua upeo wake wa kiakili, na kuboresha maandishi yake.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Despite immense fame, I was blinded and cast out from the court in my old age, dying in poverty and obscurity.
translatedText: Licha ya umaarufu mkubwa, nilipofushwa na kufukuzwa kutoka kwa mahakama katika uzee wangu, nikifa katika umaskini na kutojulikana.

sourceText: Though my life ended in tragedy, my poetry survived, laying the foundation for all Persian literature to come and earning me the title 'Adam of Poets'.
translatedText: Ingawa maisha yangu yaliishia kwa msiba, ushairi wangu ulinusurika, na kuweka msingi wa fasihi zote za Kiajemi kuja na kuniletea jina la 'Adamu wa Washairi'.

sourceText: 10th Century Samanid Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Samanid ya Karne ya 10

sourceText: Mahmud al-Kashgari
translatedText: Mahmud al-Kashgari

sourceText: Forced into exile from my homeland due to political upheaval and witnessing the gradual decline of Turkic cultural prominence in favor of Arabic.
translatedText: Kulazimishwa uhamishoni kutoka nchi yangu kwa sababu ya misukosuko ya kisiasa na kushuhudia kupungua polepole kwa umaarufu wa kitamaduni wa Kituruki kwa kupendelea Kiarabu.

sourceText: I transformed my exile into a grand ethnographic journey, traveling across Turkic lands to meticulously document their diverse languages, customs, and poetry, culminating in my magnum opus.
translatedText: Nilibadilisha uhamisho wangu kuwa safari kuu ya ethnografia, nikisafiri katika nchi za Kituruki ili kuandika kwa uangalifu lugha zao tofauti, mila, na mashairi, ikiishia kwa opus yangu kuu.

sourceText: 11th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 11

sourceText: Michael Psellos
translatedText: Michael Psellos

sourceText: Constantly navigating the treacherous currents of court politics to maintain his position and, at times, his life.
translatedText: Kupitia mikondo ya hila ya siasa za mahakama ili kudumisha msimamo wake na, wakati mwingine, maisha yake.

sourceText: He overcame political perils through his diplomatic skill, intellectual prowess, and by making himself an indispensable advisor to successive rulers.
translatedText: Alishinda hatari za kisiasa kupitia ustadi wake wa kidiplomasia, uwezo wake wa kiakili, na kwa kujifanya kuwa mshauri wa lazima kwa watawala waliofuata.

sourceText: 11th Century Byzantine Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Byzantine ya Karne ya 11

sourceText: Mikhail Lomonosov
translatedText: Mikhail Lomonosov

sourceText: Humble origins and constant opposition from foreign-dominated academic circles.
translatedText: Asili ya unyenyekevu na upinzani wa mara kwa mara kutoka kwa duru za kitaaluma zinazotawaliwa na wageni.

sourceText: Through sheer perseverance, intellectual brilliance, and the patronage of enlightened figures.
translatedText: Kupitia uvumilivu kamili, ustadi wa kiakili, na upendeleo wa takwimu zilizoelimika.

sourceText: 18th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 18

sourceText: Mykhailo Drahomanov
translatedText: Mykhailo Drahomanov

sourceText: Dismissed from my university post and forced into exile by the Tsarist regime for my political views and support for Ukrainian culture.
translatedText: Kufukuzwa kutoka wadhifa wangu wa chuo kikuu na kulazimishwa uhamishoni na utawala wa Tsarist kwa maoni yangu ya kisiasa na kuunga mkono utamaduni wa Kiukreni.

sourceText: In exile, I founded the journal 'Hromada' in Geneva, creating a hub for Ukrainian political thought and continuing to publish works that influenced generations.
translatedText: Nikiwa uhamishoni, nilianzisha jarida la 'Hromada' huko Geneva, na kuunda kitovu cha mawazo ya kisiasa ya Kiukreni na kuendelea kuchapisha kazi ambazo ziliathiri vizazi.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Mykhailo Hrushevsky
translatedText: Mykhailo Hrushevsky

sourceText: Fighting for Ukrainian identity and independence under the oppression of both Tsarist Russia and the Soviet Union, facing political exile, arrest, and the ultimate failure of his political project.
translatedText: Kupigania utambulisho wa Kiukreni na uhuru chini ya ukandamizaji wa Tsarist Russia na Umoja wa Kisovyeti, inakabiliwa na uhamisho wa kisiasa, kukamatwa, na kushindwa kwa mwisho kwa mradi wake wa kisiasa.

sourceText: He channeled his efforts into his magnum opus, the 'History of Ukraine-Rus'', to establish a distinct national narrative, and briefly realized his political vision as the head of the Central Rada, laying the groundwork for Ukrainian statehood.
translatedText: Alielekeza juhudi zake katika opus yake kuu, 'Historia ya Ukraine-Rus'', ili kuanzisha simulizi tofauti la kitaifa, na kwa ufupi akagundua maono yake ya kisiasa kama mkuu wa Rada ya Kati, akiweka msingi wa serikali ya Kiukreni.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: The repeated loss of his ancestral homeland, Samarkand, and the years spent as a landless prince in exile.
translatedText: Kupoteza mara kwa mara kwa nchi ya babu yake, Samarkand, na miaka aliyotumia kama mkuu asiye na ardhi uhamishoni.

sourceText: By turning his ambitions towards India, he conquered Delhi and established the foundations of the Mughal Empire, forging a new destiny.
translatedText: Kwa kugeuza matarajio yake kuelekea India, alishinda Delhi na kuanzisha misingi ya Dola ya Mughal, na kuunda hatima mpya.

sourceText: 15th-16th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 15-16

sourceText: Internal rebellions and constant external threats
translatedText: Uasi wa ndani na vitisho vya nje vya mara kwa mara

sourceText: Military genius, ruthless determination, and strategic alliances
translatedText: Fikra za kijeshi, azimio lisilo na huruma, na ushirikiano wa kimkakati

sourceText: Late 10th - Early 11th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 10 - Mapema karne ya 11

sourceText: Witnessing the lack of complete monastic rules (Vinaya) in China and enduring the loss of companions during his treacherous journey.
translatedText: Kushuhudia ukosefu wa sheria kamili za kimonaki (Vinaya) nchini Uchina na kuvumilia upotezaji wa wenzi wakati wa safari yake ya hiana.

sourceText: His unwavering faith and singular focus on his mission to acquire the sacred texts allowed him to persevere through immense hardship and return to China.
translatedText: Imani yake isiyoyumba na umakini wake wa pekee katika utume wake wa kupata maandiko matakatifu ulimruhusu kustahimili shida kubwa na kurudi Uchina.

sourceText: 4th-5th Century CE
translatedText: Karne ya 4-5 BK

sourceText: The constant suspicion of the Emperor and the political intrigues of the court.
translatedText: Tuhuma za mara kwa mara za Mtawala na fitina za kisiasa za korti.

sourceText: Through unwavering loyalty to the Empire and proving my worth with victories on the battlefield.
translatedText: Kupitia uaminifu usioyumba kwa Dola na kuthibitisha thamani yangu kwa ushindi kwenye uwanja wa vita.

sourceText: 6th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 6

sourceText: Bohdan Khmelnytsky
translatedText: Bohdan Khmelnytsky

sourceText: Oppression by Polish magnates, personal tragedy, and the betrayal of allies.
translatedText: Ukandamizaji wa wakuu wa Poland, janga la kibinafsi, na usaliti wa washirika.

sourceText: Uniting the Cossacks for a massive rebellion and establishing the Cossack Hetmanate through military and diplomatic means.
translatedText: Kuunganisha Cossacks kwa uasi mkubwa na kuanzisha Cossack Hetmanate kupitia njia za kijeshi na kidiplomasia.

sourceText: 17th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 17

sourceText: Bumin Qaghan
translatedText: Bumin Qaghan
